title: A resolution recognizing the partnership between the United States and Ukraine.
plain_summary: This resolution is about the friendship between the United States and Ukraine. It says that America has helped Ukraine during very hard times when Russia attacked their country illegally. The Senate supports Ukraine's right to be independent and keep all of its land. Ukraine is thankful for America's help and hopes that President Trump can help bring peace.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations. (text: CR S1584)

sections.title: Resolution Recognizing the Partnership Between the United States and Ukraine
sections.text: The Senate recognizes the partnership between the United States and Ukraine. The United States has been Ukraine's most important partner among all international allies, helping Ukraine during the most difficult times in its history. On March third, two thousand twenty-five, Ukraine's parliament called the Verkhovna Rada expressed deep thanks to President Donald Trump, Congress, and the American people. They thanked America for strong and steady support of Ukraine's independence, self-rule, and control over its own territory. They also thanked America for military aid packages that helped make the fighting more stable on the front lines. America's leadership around the world is closely connected to defending freedom and democracy, following international agreements, and being reliable with allies and friends. Our nation's security and steady growth depend on America's unwavering support and reflect the values that have made America historically successful, inspiring millions of Ukrainians. Support for Ukraine is more important than ever for the Ukrainian people and Ukraine's Security and Defense Forces, and for keeping security and stability across all of Europe. The people of Ukraine want peace and believe that President Donald Trump's personal role and his peace-making efforts will be key to quickly stopping the fighting and achieving peace for Ukraine, Europe, and the whole world. On March third, two thousand twenty-five, Ukraine's parliament said it welcomes President Donald Trump's plans to start peace talks. Ukraine's parliament has stressed the need to keep building the partnership with the United States, especially in finding and using important minerals. Ukraine's parliament has stated again that Ukraine must stay an independent and self-ruling country, and the Ukrainian people must remain free and unconquered. The Senate reaffirms America's support for Ukraine's right to rule itself and control its territory against Russia's illegal invasion. The Senate also reaffirms the friendship and shared values between the American people and allied fighting forces.
